title: Developer infrastructure overview: API surface, integration journey, and failure states
productContext: Fictional early-stage stablecoin infrastructure provider (product name 'Stablebridge') maintains a developer documentation page at developers.stablebridge.example.com that walks integration engineers and technical evaluators through the API surface, the integration journey, and what happens when a transaction fails. The product is pre-launch: there is no public uptime SLA, no published transaction volume, and no committed throughput figure. The page is developer-first (not a marketing landing) and treats honest failure handling as a feature, not an afterthought. Every URL in this brief is synthetic and uses example.com.
users: developer reading the API surface to scope an integration before requesting access
integration engineer following the step-by-step integration journey end to end
technical evaluator inspecting how Stablebridge documents and recovers from transaction failures
jobs: understand the API surface (the available resources and how they relate) without being sold a volume or uptime number
evaluate the integration journey step by step and know what to do at each stage
see explicit failure states for transactions (what fails, why, and how to recover) rather than a happy-path-only doc
leave the page able to estimate integration effort honestly, including the failure paths
requiredJourneys: developer opens the page, scans the API overview, and reaches a representative resource detail
integration engineer follows the integration journey from the first step to the final verification step
technical evaluator navigates into the error and failure-state reference and finds at least one explicit transaction-failure state with a recovery path
any visitor can reach a request-access or contact path from the docs without the page morphing into a marketing pitch
constraints: Do not claim specific transaction volumes, throughput numbers, uptime percentages, or SLAs; none are supplied in this brief and the product is pre-launch.
The integration journey must be presented as a finite, ordered sequence of steps with a clear entry and a clear verification exit; do not leave the journey open-ended.

Failure states are load-bearing for this case: every happy-path step in the integration journey must have a corresponding failure or error treatment, and at least one explicit transaction-failure state (for example a failed or reverted payment) with a documented recovery path must appear in the error reference.
Error and failure-state documentation must include actionable recovery guidance, not just an HTTP status code; a bare status code without a next action fails the case.
The page is developer-first: do not introduce marketing claims, customer logos, or volume figures that would shift the register toward sales.

Exactly one primary call to action (request access or contact developer relations) may appear; secondary navigation between the API overview, integration journey, and error reference must remain reachable without competing with it.

The product name Stablebridge is fictional; do not imply affiliation with any real stablecoin issuer, blockchain network, wallet provider, or exchange, and do not claim compatibility with a network that is not named in this brief.

mobileRules: the resource graph or list collapses to a vertically scrollable stack without losing the relationship labels
the resource-unavailable state keeps the request-access path reachable inline
the journey rail collapses to a vertical stepper that keeps the failure branch attached to its parent step
the step-failed state shows the recovery next action without pushing the developer off-screen
error rows keep the HTTP status, the human reason, and the recovery action on a single stacked block on narrow viewports
the transaction-failed-recovery state surfaces the recovery steps inline without a modal that breaks the back button
